choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — $x=\frac{7by}{11}$: This would mean $\frac{1}{7b}=\frac{11 \cdot 7by/11}{y}=7b$, giving $\frac{1}{7b}=7b$, false in general. ✗
  • Choice B — $x=y-77b$: Substituting back gives $\frac{1}{7b}=\frac{11(y-77b)}{y}$, which is not a valid identity. ✗
  • Choice C — $x=\frac{y}{77b}$: Substituting: $\frac{11x}{y}=\frac{11 \cdot y/(77b)}{y}=\frac{11}{77b}=\frac{1}{7b}$ ✓. ✓
  • Choice D — $x=77by$: Substituting: $\frac{11 \cdot 77by}{y}=847b \neq \frac{1}{7b}$ in general. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
